intro: Thailand enforces strict customs and aviation security rules. Lists below distill common trip risks — verify every item against official embassy and Customs notices.
vapeWarning: E-cigarettes, vape pens, nicotine pods/e-liquid, and related paraphernalia are illegal in Thailand. Do not carry them through Thai airports — customs confiscations, fines, deportation or arrest scenarios are routinely reported online. Leave vaping gear behind even if nicotine-only or THC-free.
carryOn: Liquids over 100 ml tubes when cabin rules apply — double-check airline and transfer airports
Loose lithium batteries/power banks improperly packed — must follow ICAO pouch rules
Tools, blades and self-defense items flagged as weapons by security theater
Large undeclared cash bundles that surpass declaration thresholds mid-flight itineraries
checkedAndCustoms: Counterfeit apparel, falsified DAO merch and pirated installers
Wildlife souvenirs, untreated wood/soil violating biosecurity
High-power radios, jamming gadgets, undeclared commercial drone kits
prohibited: Narcotics and controlled psychoactive substances (including prescription opioids without compliant paperwork)
Cannabis flower and extracts above currently published tourism carve-outs announced by MFA/MOPH
Counterfeit branded goods and pirated media
Ivory, protected wildlife trophies, shells and corals lacking CITES permits
Obscene material categories restricted under Thai criminal codes even if sold legally elsewhere
Radio transmitters, IMSI-catching hardware, scanners without Thai telecom registration
Firearms, ammunition, explosives, firearm-shaped replicas lacking explicit approvals
Fireworks or industrial chemicals routed as personal luggage without permits
restricted: Bearer instruments and cash aggregates above lawful declaration tiers — paperwork before immigration/customs desks
Gold bullion, investment jewellery and gemstones above personal-use valuations
Plants, seeds, pets, untreated animal products needing quarantine release
Prescription meds — labelled bottles plus physician correspondence for multi-month supplies
Drones, LIDAR rigs, surveying lasers — obey CAAS/CAAT registration and geofencing
Cultural artefacts, antiques or temple donations lacking lawful export dossiers
DAO hardware vaults disguised as consumer electronics without invoice/provenance paperwork
